key_drivers: FedWatch still implies a neutral-hold Fed while DXY weakened to 98.059, keeping dollar pressure supportive for commodities and non-USD FX.
Crude retains the strongest setup because the latest interpreted EIA backdrop still points to a draw and tight supply while geopolitical energy risk remains elevated.
Equity leadership is now more selective: SOX stayed positive, but the MOVE index turned higher intraday and VIX remained elevated near the top of the balanced band, capping Nasdaq conviction.
AUD and JPY remain the clearest FX expressions because the RBA is still post-hike hawkish, BoJ normalization remains intact, and China March PMI stayed in expansion territory at 50.4.

stale_sources: Atlanta Fed GDPNow remained the April 9, 2026 official update at 1.3% for 2026:Q1.
FRED DFII10, HY OAS, and T10Y2Y retain official publication lags into April 16-17 rather than intraday April 20 prints.
World Gold Council ETF detail remained partially gated, so March 2026 flow direction was carried from repository-documented evidence.
fallbacks_used: TradingView was used for DXY, SOX, and MOVE because it provided readable live direction within the browser.
Repository same-day factor notes were used to preserve the latest interpreted EIA inventory signal, copper direction, and central-bank classifications where direct page extraction was incomplete.
Search results were used to cross-check VIX, China PMI, and central-bank stance context when direct source access was blocked or partially inaccessible.
